title: Girl Finds Rare Salamander Under Bridge
summary: A 10-year-old girl named Evie found a rare Mexican axolotl under a bridge in Wales. An axolotl is a type of salamander that lives in water. Evie was playing in the river when she saw the axolotl hiding under a mat. The axolotl, named Dippy, was hurt and had damage to its tail and stomach. Evie and her mom took Dippy home to care for it. Experts say this is the first time an axolotl has been found in the wild in the UK. Axolotls are endangered, with only 50 to 1,000 left in the world. They have become popular as pets because of video games like Minecraft. Evie loves nature and often finds bugs and newts. Her mom was very surprised by the discovery. The family cut their holiday short to take Dippy to their home in Leicester. A reptile expert said Evie probably saved Dippy's life.

keywords.explanation: a type of salamander that lives in water
a small animal that looks like a lizard and lives in wet places

background_read: Axolotls are amphibians that live in water and can regrow body parts. They are native to Mexico and are critically endangered in the wild.
Article_Structure: WHO: Evie, a 10-year-old girl, and her mom Melanie.
WHAT: Evie found a rare Mexican axolotl under a bridge.
WHERE: Under the 'Dipping Bridge' at Merthyr Mawr village in Wales.
WHY: Evie loves nature and was playing in the river when she discovered it.

perspectives.description: Excited and curious about finding the axolotl.
Surprised and amazed by the discovery.
